description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white, featuring blue engine nacelles and a blue vertical stabilizer with a white logo. The aircraft has two underwing turbofan engines, swept-back low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is in a tricycle configuration, with the nose gear and main gear both visible. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows along the side for passengers. The livery includes the name 'ANADOLUJET' in red and blue lettering on the fuselage. The overall design is typical of modern short- to medium-haul commercial jets.
description: A white commercial passenger airplane with blue engines and tail, swept low-mounted wings, and a forward cockpit. The fuselage features 'ANADOLUJET' branding and multiple passenger windows.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from the rear, highlighting the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_1: A left-side profile view showing the blue engine, landing gear, cockpit windows, and 'ANADOLUJET' branding.
description_view_2: A top-down view displaying the full wingspan, both engines, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_3: A right-side profile view similar to view 1, with clear visibility of the blue tail, engine, and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units, all retractable
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows
